criteria_breakdown.z-scores-valid: always fails - testPattern 'z-scores valid' does not match test name 'all flagged anomalies have |z_score| > 2.0' (weight 0.1)

criteria_breakdown.trend-monotonic: always fails - testPattern 'trend monotonic' does not match test name 'GDP trend is mostly monotonically increasing' (weight 0.2)
criteria_breakdown.correlation-positive: always fails - testPattern 'correlation positive' does not match test name 'cyclical correlation is positive' (weight 0.2)
criteria_breakdown.correlation-range: always fails - testPattern 'correlation range' does not match test name 'cyclical correlation is in plausible range 0.5 to 1.0' (weight 0.2)

changes_made: Iteration 1: Baseline run with original verbose skill (identical to skills/timeseries-analysis/SKILL.md)

Iteration 2: Optimized SKILL.md - condensed content, added explicit output format examples, emphasized min_periods=window for rolling stats, added instruction to use python3 and avoid creating extra files
Iteration 3: Confirmation run - scores are consistent and at maximum achievable levels
notes: The model produces correct output for both tasks (verified by manually running bun tests on preserved workdirs - all 7 HP tests pass, all 6 anomaly tests pass). The score ceiling is caused by testPattern regex mismatches in task.ts: 3 HP-detrending patterns and 1 anomaly-detection pattern fail to match their corresponding test names as substrings. These are task definition bugs, not model/skill issues.
